title: Delhi Launches Lakhpati Bitiya Yojana — Rs 1 Lakh for Every Girl Child at Birth
author: Ashutosh Khulbe
excerpt: The Delhi government launched the Lakhpati Bitiya Yojana on April 2, 2026. Every girl child born in Delhi will receive Rs 1 lakh deposited in a fixed deposit at birth.
key_stat: Rs 1 lakh fixed deposit for every girl born in Delhi
body: The Delhi government launched the Lakhpati Bitiya Yojana on April 2, 2026, promising Rs 1 lakh in a fixed deposit for every girl child born in Delhi. The amount will be deposited at birth and can be withdrawn when the girl turns 18 years old.

This scheme is part of Delhi's broader push for girl child welfare, joining similar state-level initiatives like Maharashtra's Lek Ladki Yojana (Rs 98,000 over 18 years) and UP's Kanya Sumangala Yojana (Rs 25,000 in stages). Delhi's scheme stands out for its lumpsum approach — the entire Rs 1 lakh is deposited at birth rather than in installments.

To be eligible, the girl child must be born in Delhi to parents who are Delhi residents. The scheme is expected to benefit approximately 2-3 lakh girls born in Delhi every year. Registration details and application process are expected to be announced through the Delhi government's official portal.

callout.heading: Combine with Sukanya Samriddhi for maximum benefit
callout.content: Open a Sukanya Samriddhi Yojana (SSY) account alongside Lakhpati Bitiya. SSY gives 8.2% interest with EEE tax status. If parents additionally invest Rs 1.5 lakh/year in SSY, the girl gets Rs 1 lakh (Delhi scheme) + Rs 73 lakh (SSY maturity) = Rs 74 lakh by age 21.

faqs.question: Who is eligible for Delhi Lakhpati Bitiya Yojana?
faqs.answer: Girls born in Delhi to parents who are residents of Delhi. The exact eligibility criteria including income limits (if any) and documentation requirements are expected to be announced with the official notification. Check the Delhi government portal for updates.

faqs.question: When can the Rs 1 lakh be withdrawn?
faqs.answer: The fixed deposit matures when the girl turns 18 years old. At 7% FD interest over 18 years, the Rs 1 lakh could grow to approximately Rs 3.4 lakh at maturity. The exact interest rate and terms will be specified in the scheme guidelines.

faqs.question: How is this different from Sukanya Samriddhi Yojana?
faqs.answer: Lakhpati Bitiya is a one-time Rs 1 lakh deposit by the Delhi state government — parents don't contribute. SSY requires parents to deposit Rs 250-1.5 lakh per year for 15 years. Both can be availed simultaneously. SSY is a central scheme available nationwide; Lakhpati Bitiya is Delhi-specific.
